message:
feat(writing/slack-mattermost): replace ASCII decision tree with a native React decision matrix

The "Quick routing before you commit" section used an ASCII-art tree
inside a <Code> block. It read fine in a monospace terminal, but on
the article page it was functionally a code dump: 45 lines of box-
drawing characters that wrapped awkwardly on mobile, didn't respect
the article's purple / cyan / emerald palette, couldn't link to the
sections it referenced, and were effectively unreadable to screen
readers.

This commit replaces the ASCII block with a proper DecisionMatrix
component: four question cards, each with three routing branches,
each branch rendered as a structured row with a real anchor link to
the section it points at.

------------------------------------------------------------
 Layout
------------------------------------------------------------

- Single column on mobile, single column on desktop (each card is
  wide enough that stacking them vertically reads better than two
  columns at 1200px+).
- Each card:
    - Numbered badge (1–4) in the card's accent color.
    - Question header in the same accent, uppercase-mono eyebrow
      styling.
    - A soft vertical rail on the left in the accent color, to read
      as "same tree" rather than four disconnected panels.
    - Three branch rows:
        - Left: the answer label (e.g. "Free or Pro"), bold, with a
          directional arrow in the accent color.
        - Right: an accent-colored outcome pill (e.g.
          "Track B · slackdump-primary") above the descriptive body.
        - Body text uses the article's existing Mono helper for
          technical identifiers (POSTGRES_DSN, TARGET_HOST) and a
          DecisionLink helper that anchors directly to the section it
          references (§3, §Grid-migration, §2.3, Part 1).
- Each of the four cards gets a different accent color so the tree
  visually sections itself as the reader scrolls:
    - 1 Slack plan ....... purple
    - 2 Host ............. cyan
    - 3 Agent driver ..... emerald
    - 4 Database ......... amber

------------------------------------------------------------
 Content preserved verbatim, just re-flowed into structure
------------------------------------------------------------

Every original branch is kept. The ASCII version was already
authoritative on the routing rules, so the matrix carries exactly
the same information, plus a couple of clarifying sentences that
the ASCII block didn't have room for (e.g. why the Supabase
*session pooler* on 5432 is the right choice and not the
*transaction pooler* on 6543; that TARGET_HOST is how the BYO-host
branch gets wired).

------------------------------------------------------------
 Components
------------------------------------------------------------

Added inline in components/slack-migration-article.tsx, next to the
existing article helpers (Code / Mono / Sub / SectionHeader):

- DecisionMatrix    — the four-card layout
- DecisionCard      — one accent-colored card with number + question
- DecisionBranchRow — a single routing branch row
- DecisionPill      — an accent-colored outcome pill
- DecisionLink      — a styled anchor to another section
- DECISION_ACCENT   — the palette map (purple / cyan / emerald /
                      amber), so a single Tailwind-safe object drives
                      the border, bg, text, num-badge, rail, pill bg,
                      pill border, and pill text for each accent

Tailwind's JIT needs string-literal class names to prune correctly.
The accent map is hand-written per color and every class name lives
verbatim in source, so no dynamic concatenation defeats the JIT.

------------------------------------------------------------
 Verified
------------------------------------------------------------

- bunx eslint components/slack-migration-article.tsx: clean
- bun run build: exit 0, article still prerendered as static (○)
- Screenshotted via Playwright at 1280x1400 desktop and 390x900
  mobile: all four cards render with correct accent colors, anchor
  links are clickable, mobile stacks label above pill+body cleanly,
  no layout overflow, no color palette surprises.

files.patch:
@@ -260,6 +260,326 @@ function Sub({ id, eyebrow, children }: { id?: string; eyebrow?: string; childre ); } +// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── +// Decision matrix: 4 question cards, each with 3 routing branches. +// Replaces an ASCII-art tree so screen readers + mobile work nicely. +// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── +type DecisionAccent = "purple" | "cyan" | "emerald" | "amber"; + +type DecisionBranch = { + answer: ReactNode; + pill?: ReactNode; + body: ReactNode; +}; + +type DecisionCardData = { + num: string; + accent: DecisionAccent; + question: string; + branches: DecisionBranch[]; +}; + +function DecisionMatrix() { + const cards: DecisionCardData[] = [ + { + num: "1", + accent: "purple", + question: "What Slack plan are you on?", + branches: [ + { + answer: "Free or Pro", + pill: Track B · slackdump-primary, + body: ( + <> + See §3. Expect + public channels plus the private channels and DMs your + authenticated account is a member of. Other people’s DMs are + invisible. + + ), + }, + { + answer: "Business+", + pill: Track A · official admin export, + body: ( + <> + See §3. Full + workspace content, including private channels and every user’s + DMs. + + ), + }, + { + answer: "Enterprise Grid", + pill: Track C · grid split, + body: ( + <> + See{" "} + §Grid-migration. + Either grid-wide export plus a per-workspace split, or + per-workspace exports. + + ), + }, + ], + }, + { + num: "2", + accent: "cyan", + question: "Where will Mattermost live?", + branches: [ + { + answer: "Hetzner AX42/AX52 dedicated", + pill: recommended, + body: ( + <> + Sizing in{" "} + §2.3. AX42 + comfortably serves ~250 users; AX52 handles 1,000. + + ), + }, + { + answer: "OVH Advance / Contabo VPS", + pill: cheaper, + body: <>Same sizing table, different vendor. Lower cost, slightly less headroom., + }, + { + answer: "Existing infra you run", + pill: BYO host, + body: ( + <> + Supply the SSH target as TARGET_HOST; the skill + provisions on top of whatever Ubuntu box you point it at. + + ), + }, + ], + }, + { + num: "3", + accent: "emerald", + question: "How do you want to drive the agent?", + branches: [ + { + answer: "Click, don’t type", + pill: Path A · Desktop app, + body: ( + <> + See Part 1. Claude + Code or Codex desktop app plus jsm. + + ), + }, + { + answer: "Terminal-native", + pill: Path B · CLI only, + body: <>Leanest install. Claude Code or Codex CLI, skills symlinked or jsm-installed., + }, + { + answer: "Multi-machine", + pill: Path C · CLI + jsm sync, + body: ( + <> + Cross-device skill sync via jsm sync. Right when + two different laptops drive the same migration at different + stages. + + ), + }, + ], + }, + { + num: "4", + accent: "amber", + question: "Where is your Mattermost database going to live?", + branches: [ + { + answer: "Same box as Mattermost", + pill: default, + body: ( + <> + {"POSTGRES_DSN=postgres://…@localhost:5432"}. Skill + provisions local PostgreSQL for you. + + ), + }, + { + answer: "Supabase (managed)", + pill: managed PG, + body: ( + <> + Supabase session pooler{" "} + on 5432. Not the transaction pooler on 6543 — the + import job needs session state. + + ), + }, + { + answer: "Your own managed PG", + pill: BYO DB, + body: ( + <> + Provide the DSN; the skill is hands-off on provisioning and only + reads/writes to what you hand it. + + ), + }, + ], + }, + ]; + + return ( +

+ ); +} + +function DecisionPill({ + accent, + children, +}: { + accent: DecisionAccent; + children: ReactNode; +}) { + const cls = DECISION_ACCENT[accent]; + return ( + + {children} + + ); +} + +function DecisionLink({ href, children }: { href: string; children: ReactNode }) { + return ( + + {children} + + ); +} + export function SlackMigrationArticle() { const [scrollProgress, setScrollProgress] = useState(0); const articleRef = useRef(null); @@ -725,48 +1045,8 @@ export function SlackMigrationArticle() { Quick routing before you commit - -{`┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ -│ What Slack plan are you on? │ -└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ - │ - ├── Free or Pro ──────→ Track B (slackdump-primary). See §3. - │ Expect: public + accessible private channels - │ and DMs only. Other people's DMs are invisible. - │ - ├── Business+ ───────→ Track A (official admin export). See §3. - │ Expect: full workspace content. - │ - └── Enterprise Grid ─→ Track C (grid split). See §Grid-migration. - Either grid-wide export + split, or - per-workspace exports. - -┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ -│ Where will Mattermost live? │ -└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ - │ - ├── Hetzner AX42/AX52 dedicated (recommended) ─→ §2.3 sizing - ├── OVH Advance / Contabo VPS ─────────────────→ same table, cheaper - └── Existing infra you run ─────────────────────→ supply SSH target - -┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ -│ How do you want to drive the agent? │ -└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ - │ - ├── Click, don't type ───→ Path A (Desktop app). See §Part 1. - ├── Terminal-native ────→ Path B (CLI-only). - └── Multi-machine ──────→ Path C (CLI + jsm for cross-device sync). - -┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ -│ Where is your Mattermost database going to live? │ -└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ - │ - ├── Same box as Mattermost (default) → POSTGRES_DSN=postgres://…@localhost:5432 - │ Skill provisions local PG for you. - ├── Supabase (managed) → Supabase session pooler on 5432 - │ (NOT the transaction pooler on 6543). - └── Your own managed PG → Provide the DSN; skill is hands-off.`} - + +

{"If any branch surprises you, read its linked section before continuing. The skills themselves detect which path the operator is on and route accordingly; the Slack plan tier alone changes validators downstream."}
